perturbation.original_text: The Professional hereby grants to the Company an exclusive license to use her name, likeness, image and personal identification, singly or in any combination, in connection with the production, use, marketing and sale of a "Kathy Whitworth" signature line of women's golf clubs (the "Products"), as described more fully in Section 3 below.
perturbation.changed_text: The Professional hereby grants to the Company a non-exclusive license to use her name, likeness, image and personal identification, singly or in any combination, in connection with the production, use, marketing and sale of a "Kathy Whitworth" signature line of women's golf clubs (the "Products"), as described more fully in Section 3 below.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' in Section 2.1 creates a contradiction with Section 3.1, which grants an 'exclusive' license. This introduces uncertainty about the exclusivity of the license granted to the Company, potentially allowing the Professional to endorse competing products, which would undermine the purpose of the endorsement agreement. The in-text contradiction of the license being exclusive in one section and non-exclusive in another section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Professional hereby grants an exclusive, worldwide license to the Company to use the name, likeness, image and personal identification of the Professional, during the Term and for a period of six (6) months after the Term as provided in Section 2.8, in connection with the creation, manufacture, marketing, sale and promotion of the Products. As a condition precedent to, and a continuing precedent of, any obligations of the Company hereunder, the Professional hereby agrees to use the Products upon their creation and to provide an unqualified and unequivocal endorsement thereof during the Term at the request of the Company at any time or times during the Term in verbal, written or recorded forms. If the Professional is unable at any time during the Term to provide such endorsement of the Products, the Company shall be released from any of its obligations under Sections 4.1, 4.2, and 4.3 hereof to pay any fees or royalties or to provide any stock options to the Professional and may elect to terminate this Agreement without any further obligation to the Professional.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' directly contradicts the explicitly stated 'exclusive' license granted in the contradicted section. This creates a clear and material inconsistency regarding the company's rights, undermining the core terms of the agreement and potentially leading to disputes. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities' definition of in-text contradiction where key terms (exclusivity) are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall cease use of the name, likeness, image or personal identification of the Professional upon expiration or termination of this Agreement. However, the Company will have the right to dispose of its inventory of Products existing at the time of termination or expiration of this Agreement and the right to use the name, likeness, image and personal identification of the Professional in connection with the disposition of such inventory. The right granted in this section shall expire six (6) months after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall cease use of the name, likeness, image or personal identification of the Professional upon expiration or termination of this Agreement. However, the Company will have the right to dispose of its inventory of Products existing at the time of termination or expiration of this Agreement and the right to use the name, likeness, image and personal identification of the Professional in connection with the disposition of such inventory. The right granted in this section shall expire twelve (12) months after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the expiration period from 'six (6) months' to 'twelve (12) months' after termination in Section 2.8 contradicts the language found later in Section 3.1. This creates ambiguity as to how long the Company can use the Professional's image and likeness after the agreement ends for disposing inventory. The in-text contradiction of the post-termination rights duration causes a conflict over when the company has to stop using her image or personal identification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Professional hereby grants an exclusive, worldwide license to the Company to use the name, likeness, image and personal identification of the Professional, during the Term and for a period of six (6) months after the Term as provided in Section 2.8, in connection with the creation, manufacture, marketing, sale and promotion of the Products. As a condition precedent to, and a continuing precedent of, any obligations of the Company hereunder, the Professional hereby agrees to use the Products upon their creation and to provide an unqualified and unequivocal endorsement thereof during the Term at the request of the Company at any time or times during the Term in verbal, written or recorded forms. If the Professional is unable at any time during the Term to provide such endorsement of the Products, the Company shall be released from any of its obligations under Sections 4.1, 4.2, and 4.3 hereof to pay any fees or royalties or to provide any stock options to the Professional and may elect to terminate this Agreement without any further obligation to the Professional.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration the Company has to use the Professional's name and likeness after the Term from six (6) months to twelve (12) months in Section 2.8. This directly contradicts the statement in Section 3.1 that states the license is granted 'for a period of six (6) months after the Term as provided in Section 2.8'. This is a clear inconsistency in deadlines as described in the definition for Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction. This difference could lead to disputes over the Company's rights after the initial six-month period.

perturbation.original_text: The Professional agrees to use only the golf clubs and golf bags of the Company in any golf event, whether professional or social, during the Term. The Professional agrees (i) to use no golf bag bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company and (ii) to wear no apparel bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company, and will prohibit any caddy of hers from bearing any such identification.
perturbation.changed_text: The Professional agrees to make reasonable efforts to use the golf clubs and golf bags of the Company in any golf event, whether professional or social, during the Term. The Professional agrees (i) to use no golf bag bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company and (ii) to wear no apparel bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company, and will prohibit any caddy of hers from bearing any such identification.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to use only' to 'agrees to make reasonable efforts to use' introduces a contradiction with the restrictions on competitors' apparel and equipment in the same section and other sections that rely on her exclusive endorsement. Section 2.7 creates an ambiguity around whether the professional can use golf clubs or bags from a competitor, especially if ‘reasonable efforts’ fail. This weakens the obligation on the Professional to exclusively use the Company's products. The in-text contradiction weakens the original agreement, as now the exclusivity and dedication to using only the golf clubs is in question.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Professional acknowledges that any use of her name, likeness, image or personal identification by any third party in connection with the making, use, sale, marketing, promotion or advertising of golf equipment, including but not limited to golf clubs and golf bags, would cause a likelihood of confusion with the Products of the Company, during the Term and thereafter during the time the Company disposes of inventory on hand at the expiration of this Agreement. The Professional acknowledges that she will have a right, pursuant to and under the conditions described in Section 4.2 above, to receive a specified royalty for inventory on hand at the expiration of the initial term, and accordingly hereby grants to the Company the right to fill any orders for, assemble components of, market, advertise, promote and sell any inventory of Products in its inventory existing at the expiration or termination of this Agreement, for a period not to exceed two (2) years after such expiration or termination of the original term. To avoid any possibility of confusion of the public, trademark infringement or interference with the rights of the Company, the Professional agrees not to endorse, license or otherwise authorize the use of her name, likeness or image in connection with another company's golf clubs or golf-related clothing or equipment during the Term and for a period of two (2) years thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Professional 'agrees to use only' the Company's golf clubs and bags. The changed text modifies this to 'agrees to make reasonable efforts to use' the Company's equipment. This introduces ambiguity, creating a loophole where the Professional might use competitor equipment under the guise of 'reasonable efforts' failing. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which prohibits the Professional from endorsing or using any other company's golf clubs or equipment. The change weakens the exclusivity agreement and creates a potential conflict, making it unclear whether the Professional is bound by the exclusivity clause or can use competitor equipment under the 'reasonable efforts' exception. This weakens the company's position against trademark infringement, directly contradicting the explicit terms in the 'Contradicted Text'.
